title: Render-pool warmup + unification + Kaboom Crew polish r3

stories.title: runtime.renderer.pools() lists every render pool's live + peak
stories.summary: Surface a programmatic accessor on the renderer so an agent can ask which pools exist + how loaded they are. Mirrors AssetRegistry.inventory() shape. The adapter (`ThreeRenderAdapter`) already owns three RenderPoolRegistry instances (instanced, batched, particle); add a `pools(): ReadonlyArray<{ name; live; peak }>` method on the adapter and forward via `ThreeRenderer.pools()`. Includes peak-tracking on RenderPoolRegistry so the value survives release.
verification: runtime.renderer.pools() returns ReadonlyArray<{ name: 'instanced'|'batched'|'particle'; live: number; peak: number }>
RenderPoolRegistry gains peak() + reset(); acquire bumps peak; release does NOT decrease it; reset() drops counter+peak
unit test exercises acquire/release/peak retention + reset

stories.title: GET /__agf/pool-inventory exposes the same shape over the dev bridge
stories.summary: Mirror the S86 asset-inventory probe: dev bridge route `/__agf/pool-inventory` returns JSON identical to runtime.renderer.pools(). Lets the live-debug Playwright skill grab pool state in a single curl. Documented in docs/agent-probes.md.
verification: GET /__agf/pool-inventory returns ReadonlyArray<{ name, live, peak }>
page-bridge.ts forwards window.__agf.poolInventory(); agf-dev-bridge maps /pool-inventory → pool-inventory channel
docs/agent-probes.md adds a /__agf/pool-inventory section beside asset/invalidate

stories.title: ParticleEmitterSystem accepts `preWarmPresets` for engine-level warmup
stories.summary: S85 AGF-POOL-WARMUP-PARTICLES shipped as a project-local hack (kaboom-crew spawns a hidden offscreen ParticleEmitter on attachUi to force shader compile). Promote it: ParticleEmitterSystem now takes `preWarmPresets?: ReadonlyArray<string>` + `preWarmFrames?: number`; on the first frame of every world it acquires a 1-capacity offscreen pool per preset (forcing shader compile + GPU buffer upload), then releases them after `preWarmFrames` (default 4) frames so they don't linger in the live count. RuntimeOptions exposes `particlePreWarmPresets`, plumbed from project.json's `render.particlePreWarmPresets`.
verification: +6 unit tests on the system stub adapter: empty preset list is a no-op; one acquire per preset; release after preWarmFrames; peak survives release; world-swap re-arms the warmup
RuntimeOptions.particlePreWarmPresets forwards to createParticleEmitterSystem
src/app.ts forwards project.json#render.particlePreWarmPresets
schemas/project.schema.json adds particlePreWarmPresets enum spark|glow|pulse

stories.title: engine doctor surfaces a Pools: section
stories.summary: Read the project's render-pool inventory at doctor-run time and print a 'Pools:' section: per pool, `name  live/capacity (peak N)`. Recommendation when a particle preset has `peak === 0` after >1 s of runtime (warmup ran but the project never emitted that preset — dead preset). Doctor unit test confirms the section renders for a fixture inventory.
verification: DoctorReport.pools populated from runtime.pools.inventory() snapshot taken via the doctor probe path
format pass prints a 'Pools:' section
recommendation when preWarm preset has peak=0 after 1 s of runtime

stories.title: Unit test for RenderPoolRegistry peak + reset tracking
stories.summary: RenderPoolRegistry ships acquire/release but no test covers the peak-tracking invariant introduced by AGF-POOL-INVENTORY-API. Add: acquire bumps peak; release does NOT decrease peak; reset() drops to 0; drain() does NOT touch peak.
verification: +4 unit tests under 'S88 AGF-POOL-INVENTORY-API peak tracking' describe block; existing RenderPoolRegistry suite stays green

stories.title: kaboom-crew drops the project-local warmup entity
stories.summary: Once AGF-PARTICLE-PREWARM-SYSTEM lands the project-local kaboom.warmup-particles entity is redundant. Delete the entity creation + the runtime.applyCommands hack from attachUi; kaboom-crew now relies entirely on the engine-level preWarmPresets option. Smaller bootstrap.ts; one less moving part.
verification: examples/kaboom-crew/bootstrap.ts no longer issues the kaboom.warmup-particles entity.create command
examples/kaboom-crew/project.json#render.particlePreWarmPresets = ['spark', 'glow']
engine:check stays green for kaboom-crew with the new field

stories.title: Short procedural chime on match win/lost/draw
stories.summary: When matchPhase flips from in-progress to won/lost/draw, audio-binding-system fires a new `match-end` AudioEventKind. audio-fx.ts adds three short procedural chimes (triumph triad / descending minor / neutral perfect-fifth). Inherits the existing volume dial. Defaults to one play per match resolution.
verification: audio-binding-system emits 'match-end' exactly once per matchPhase transition out of in-progress
audio-fx.ts plays a different chord for won / lost / draw
+1 unit test in audio-binding-system.test.ts covers the transition edge

stories.title: Bot pathfinding penalises cells covered by live blasts + about-to-explode bombs
stories.summary: Today's BotAISystem treats BlastTile cells as walkable, so the bot regularly suicides into its own blast on the wide map. Extend the cost function in agent-goto-system (or a small wrapper inside bot-ai-system) so cells containing a BlastTile OR a Bomb with fuseRemaining < 0.6 are treated as high-cost (e.g. cost 10 vs. 1). Pure cost bump — keeps the existing BFS; no new components.
verification: unit test: bot starting next to a live BlastTile picks a path that detours around it
unit test: bot starting next to a fuseRemaining=0.3 bomb prefers the longer non-explosive path
live probe: 60 s of bot-vs-bot at hard difficulty shows the bot suicide rate drop

stories.title: scheduler.register emits an info-level AGF_SCHEDULER_REGISTER diagnostic
stories.summary: Engine lifecycle traces follow-up from S082 — partially shipped via AGF_SCENE_LOAD_APPLIED + AGF_ENTITY_RECREATED. Closes the loop by making scheduler.register emit `info` AGF_SCHEDULER_REGISTER { systemName, phase, profile } so a snapshot reconstructs the boot-time system list without inspecting the scheduler object. No-ops in production via the diagnostics severity filter (info-level diagnostics already dropped under the default warning gate).
verification: Scheduler.register pipes through diagnostics when a bus is supplied; emission count == registered systems
+1 unit test exercises register/unregister + asserts the diagnostic ledger
boot-noise budget regression check stays green (info-level diagnostics filtered out)
